metadata.title:
SEWER CONSTRUCTION


A MENACE TO LIVES

pages.raw_text:
of gravel prevent a passage of teams
in meeting.


The danger to pedestrians in IL is
crossing is great. Cars from both
directions line up and are blocked by
freight trains. Teams congregate so
thickly that it is impossible to break
through the mass.


The mud is six inches deep at a
point where the street cars take on
passengers, who are forced to wade.


Another danger is the noise created
by the switching of engines prevent
ing pedestrians hearing approaching
wagons


Every hour in the day men, wom-
en- and children are seen fleeing for
their lives through mire and slush to
escape the trains, street cars, teams
and wheelmen. The Northern Pacific
fiagman seems powerless to keep the
crossing clear.


If something is not done soon,
damage suit against the city will be
the result, as accidents are sure to
follow if the street is allowed to
remain in it's present condition.


When Commissioner HandIey was
notified and asked to remedy the
matter he said the work was let on
contract to Peter Costello and that
he had nothing to say about it.
